---
title: "CombinedProbs"
author: "Daniel Myung"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# 1. LOAD BOTH TEST SETS
# We use the original for the labels and the dummy for alignment checks
test_raw   <- readRDS("test_original.rds")
test_dummy <- readRDS("test_dummy.rds")

# 2. EXTRACT THE TARGET (The "Actual" Truth)
# It's better to pull the factor version (Yes/No) from the original file
# so your Meta-Model output is human-readable.
y_true <- test_raw$History.of.Mental.Illness

# 3. LOAD ALL 6 PROBABILITY VECTORS
p_logreg <- readRDS("glm_prob.rds")
p_knn    <- readRDS("knn_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_ann    <- readRDS("ann_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_svm    <- readRDS("best_svm_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_tree   <- readRDS("c50_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_rf     <- readRDS("rf_prob_mental_illness.rds") # New addition!

# 4. COMBINE INTO THE STACK
# This is the "Usable Format" your Random Forest Meta-Model will train on
stack_df <- data.frame(
  logreg = as.numeric(p_logreg),
  knn    = as.numeric(p_knn),
  ann    = as.numeric(p_ann),
  svm    = as.numeric(p_svm),
  tree   = as.numeric(p_tree),
  rf     = as.numeric(p_rf),
  actual = as.factor(y_true)
)

# 5. THE "DANIEL CHECK" (Crucial Step)
# If these lengths don't match, your stack is broken.
lengths <- c(length(p_logreg), length(p_knn), length(p_ann), 
             length(p_svm), length(p_tree), length(p_rf), length(y_true))

if(length(unique(lengths)) > 1) {
  stop("ALARM: Your model vectors have different lengths! Check your test sets.")
} else {
  cat("All vectors aligned at length:", lengths[1], "\n")
}

# 6. SAVE FOR LEVEL 2
saveRDS(stack_df, "depression_stacked_data.rds")

# Quick look at the 'Council of Experts' consensus
head(stack_df, 10)
```
